title: Wire classify.get_classification through CacheStore (L2)
description: Mirror TASK-013 for classify. _class_cache is L1; CacheStore.classification is L2. Warm-path is microseconds; cold-path computes and populates both.
acceptanceCriteria: classify.get_classification(content, db) returns identical dict to v0 on miss; same dict on warm hit.
Warm classify rate >= 500 tracks/sec (validated in TASK-004 benchmark).
ANLZ-missing tracks return None on warm path without re-reading.
tests/test_classify.py adds L2 hit / miss-and-populate / sentinel cases.
conftest fixture clears CacheStore.classification between tests.

steps.details: Module-level _cache_store: CacheStore | None = None. Setter called from serve/deps.py.
Order: L1 (_class_cache by content.ID) -> L2 (CacheStore.get_classification(content.ID, expected_anlz_mtime)) -> compute. On compute, write to L1 and L2 with anlz_mtime.

Classification result is {primary, scores: dict[str,float], bpm, energy_mean}. CacheStore.put_classification serializes scores via json.dumps; get_classification deserializes via json.loads. Other fields are primitives.
tests/test_classify.py: cover the L1/L2/miss matrix as in TASK-013.

technicalNotes: Classification depends on get_energy_curve (TASK-013) - the L2 hits compound: classify cache hit avoids both compute paths.
MIN_SCORE = 0.70 for auto-tag application is unchanged - the cache stores raw scores; the threshold lives in auto_tag.py.
